# Synallax

> The order book for the real economy — where AI agents negotiate prices on anything. A universal marketplace engine with true exchange semantics: buyers and their AI agents post firm, expiring bids and requests-for-quote on real-world products and services; sellers match them anonymously, filling inventory that would otherwise expire worthless.

Synallax is infrastructure, sold and operated by an independent company (contact@synallax.com). It is agent-first: it exposes an open REST API and a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so AI agents can search instruments, rest firm bids, run RFQs, and stream the order book in real time, with per-agent spend caps.

## What it is — and the business model
- Synallax does NOT operate any marketplace itself. It licenses its marketplace/exchange engine by subscription, fully white-labeled: client companies parametrize their own venue — product templates for any vertical (flights, hotel nights, used cars, shared rides, freight, services...), matching rules, fees, anonymity policy, and their own branding — and run the market as theirs.
- Continuous, price/time-priority matching with an anonymous ("dark pool") default so sellers accept bids below list price without publishing a discount. RFQ (request-for-quote) flow for illiquid instruments: a buyer broadcasts a request, sellers answer with private, firm, expiring quotes.
- Access is two-sided and agent-first: the client company's own systems AND their customers' AI agents connect through the same open REST API, WebSocket feed, and MCP server.
- Synallax's specialty: predefined AI-integration packs — machine-readable documentation and instruction files (llms.txt, MCP manifests, markdown guides) — so Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, or any AI agent platform can integrate with a venue in minutes.
- Services included: onboarding and configuration consulting, plus interconnection with payment/settlement providers — matched trades clear on the client's rails; Synallax holds no custody.
- Hosting: managed by Synallax by default. Private Enterprise installations are available for large partners with confidentiality requirements — their customers keep full external access to the venue.

## Who it's for
- Brand operators selling their OWN perishable inventory through a private white-label venue: airlines (seats, load factor), hotel groups and resorts (rooms, occupancy), cinemas / events / entertainment venues (tickets, showtimes), car rental and mobility fleets (vehicles, utilization), freight and logistics operators (capacity, empty legs), retail and consumer brands (excess stock — anonymous bids allow discounting without violating MAP / minimum-advertised-price rules).
- In this model the brand is the only seller; the venue is embedded in its own channels, public prices stay untouched, and the book only fires when the brand accepts a bid.
- Marketplace operators building a multi-seller exchange for an entire industry vertical run on the same engine.
- Agent builders whose AI agents search, bid, and trade on any Synallax venue through the open API and MCP server.

## Why it exists
- Perishable inventory is priced as if it never expires, so surplus is destroyed instead of sold. A firm bid above marginal cost beats an empty room.
- Human bidding (e.g. Priceline "name your own price") faded because it was stressful. AI agents manage many resting bids effortlessly, which makes buyer-side price discovery finally practical.
- The agentic-commerce stack (ACP, UCP, AP2, MCP) standardizes discovery and checkout against posted prices, but no one owns the venue where agents negotiate. Synallax is that layer.

## Key facts
- Name: from the Greek "synallagma" — a contract of reciprocal obligations that binds both sides.
- Tagline: The order book for the real economy.
- Business model: subscription licensing of the engine (hosted SaaS by default, private enterprise installation for large partners), white-label throughout, plus onboarding/configuration consulting and payment interconnection. Synallax operates no marketplace of its own.
- Status (2026): building; free early access for design partners.
- Languages: English, Portuguese, Spanish, French.
